name: hummingbot
description: Hummingbot trading bot framework - automated trading strategies, market making, arbitrage, connectors for crypto exchanges. Use when working with algorithmic trading, crypto trading bots, or exchange integrations.
capabilities: Provide guidance on Hummingbot installation and configuration across Docker and source methods
Explain trading strategy templates including PMM, arbitrage, XEMM, and directional strategies
Describe exchange connector setup for centralized and decentralized exchanges
Detail Gateway DEX integration for trading on Uniswap, Jupiter, Raydium, and other DEXs
Explain controller-based strategy architecture using V2 framework
Describe AI integration via Hummingbot MCP Server for Claude, Gemini, and Codex
author: 2025Emma
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown files extracted from official Hummingbot documentation. All 2092 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains no executable code, scripts, or network operations. Patterns flagged (shell commands, URLs, crypto terminology) are legitimate documentation artifacts describing trading bot setup and configuration.
valueStatement: Hummingbot is an open-source algorithmic trading framework for cryptocurrency markets. This skill provides expert guidance on deploying trading bots, configuring exchange connectors, implementing market making and arbitrage strategies, and integrating with AI agents via MCP.
limitations: Does not execute trades or manage funds directly
Does not provide financial advice or trading recommendations
Does not access user exchange accounts or API keys
Cannot install or configure Hummingbot on user systems

useCases.title: Deploy market making bots
useCases.description: Configure automated market making strategies on centralized and decentralized crypto exchanges to provide liquidity.

useCases.title: Build custom strategies
useCases.description: Create Python-based trading scripts using Hummingbot's V2 controller framework for backtested algorithmic trading.

useCases.title: Integrate AI agents
useCases.description: Connect Claude, Gemini, or Codex to Hummingbot via MCP for AI-assisted crypto trading automation.

promptTemplates.title: Getting started
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I install Hummingbot using Docker and connect it to Binance for automated trading?

promptTemplates.title: Configure strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the Pure Market Making strategy configuration with bid spread, ask spread, and order refresh time parameters.

promptTemplates.title: DEX trading
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure Gateway to trade on Jupiter DEX using a Solana wallet?

promptTemplates.title: Custom script
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a custom directional trading script using the RSI indicator with PositionExecutor.

bestPractices: Always test new strategies in paper trading mode before deploying real capital
Use balance limits and kill switches to prevent excessive losses
Monitor bot performance regularly and adjust parameters based on market conditions
Keep API keys secure and never share them; Hummingbot encrypts credentials locally
antiPatterns: Running trading bots without understanding the strategy logic or risk parameters
Leaving bots unattended without proper monitoring or kill switches configured
Using excessive leverage or order sizes that exceed your risk tolerance
Neglecting to backtest strategies on historical data before live deployment

faq.question: Which exchanges does Hummingbot support?
faq.answer: Hummingbot supports 30+ exchanges including Binance, Coinbase, OKX, Hyperliquid for CEX trading, and Uniswap, Jupiter, Raydium for DEX trading.

faq.question: What are the system requirements for running Hummingbot?
faq.answer: Docker installation requires Docker Desktop or Docker Engine. Source installation needs Python 3.10+, conda environment, and 4GB+ RAM for compilation.

faq.question: How do I connect AI assistants to Hummingbot?
faq.answer: Use the Hummingbot MCP Server to connect Claude, Gemini, or Codex. Configure in Claude settings with server command and path to hummingbot-mcp directory.

faq.question: Is my data and API keys safe with Hummingbot?
faq.answer: Yes, Hummingbot stores credentials encrypted locally. Passwords and private keys are never sent to external servers. Disable anonymized metrics in config if desired.

faq.question: Why is my bot not placing orders?
faq.answer: Check balance limits, ensure API keys have trading permissions, verify exchange connectivity with connect command, and review logs in /logs folder for errors.

faq.question: How does Hummingbot compare to other trading bots?
faq.answer: Hummingbot is open-source, community-governed, and highly customizable. Unlike closed-source bots, you can inspect all code and create custom strategies in Python.
